title: docs: add a first-contribution quickstart transcript
body:
## Goal
Add a short terminal transcript that shows a new contributor how to install AgentPack locally, run one focused test, and open a small PR.

## Suggested files
- CONTRIBUTING.md
- docs/development.md

## Acceptance criteria
- Shows setup with `python -m pip install -e ".[dev]"`.
- Shows one narrow pytest command and one ruff command.
- Explains what to paste in a PR under Validation.
- Keeps the docs concise and avoids release-only workflow details.

title: docs: add a glossary for route, pack, guard, MCP, and benchmark
body:
## Goal
Add a small glossary for common AgentPack terms used across the README and docs.

## Suggested files
- docs/index.md or docs/how-agentpack-works.md

## Acceptance criteria
- Defines route, pack, guard, MCP, benchmark, context pack, and freshness.
- Links to the full command docs where useful.
- Uses plain language for first-time contributors.

title: cli: add a help-output regression test for agentpack route
body:
## Goal
Add a focused test that checks `agentpack route --help` documents `--task`, `--json`, and `--format`.

## Suggested files
- tests/test_route_command.py

## Acceptance criteria
- Uses `CliRunner`.
- Asserts the help command exits with code 0.
- Asserts the three flags are present.
- Does not snapshot the full help output.

title: cli: improve invalid route format error text
body:
## Goal
Make the error for `agentpack route --format bad` point users at `--json` and `--format json`.

## Suggested files
- src/agentpack/commands/route.py
- tests/test_route_command.py

## Acceptance criteria
- Invalid formats still exit non-zero.
- Error mentions valid formats and the `--json` alias.
- Existing `--format json` behavior remains unchanged.

title: docs: add route JSON examples to agent integration docs
body:
## Goal
Add `agentpack route --task "..." --json` examples to integration docs that discuss scriptable or MCP-style routing.

## Suggested files
- docs/agent-plugins.md
- docs/claude-code-context-engine.md
- docs/agentpack-for-ai-agents.md

## Acceptance criteria
- Examples use `--json`, not only `--format json`.
- Docs say JSON output is for scripts/tools.
- No benchmark or product claims are added.

title: benchmark: document how to add one public benchmark case
body:
## Goal
Add a contributor-friendly checklist for adding one public benchmark case.

## Suggested files
- docs/benchmarking.md
- benchmarks/README.md

## Acceptance criteria
- Explains expected files, task text, and validation command.
- Explains that file-selection benchmarks are not task-success proof.
- Links to the current public result file.

title: benchmark: add a small regression fixture for over-selection noise
body:
## Goal
Add one small fixture or benchmark case that catches a noisy selection pattern without tuning to one repository name.

## Suggested files
- tests/test_ranking_evals.py
- tests/fixtures/ or benchmark fixture helpers

## Acceptance criteria
- Test fails before the intended ranking improvement.
- The scenario is general, not named after one benchmark repo.
- The test documents why the noisy file should not outrank the target file.

title: python: expand mypy coverage to one small analysis module
body:
## Goal
Add one low-risk module from `src/agentpack/analysis/` to the scoped mypy gate.

## Suggested files
- pyproject.toml
- selected analysis module
- focused tests if behavior changes are needed

## Acceptance criteria
- Adds exactly one module to `[tool.mypy].files`.
- `python -m mypy` passes.
- Any code changes are type-safety fixes, not broad refactors.

title: testing: cover Go workspace replace blocks with multiple requires
body:
## Goal
Add a focused test for Go workspace dependency edge detection when `require (...)` and `replace (...)` blocks contain multiple modules.

## Suggested files
- tests/test_monorepo.py

## Acceptance criteria
- Includes at least two local modules and one external module.
- Asserts only local workspace edges are returned.
- Does not require running Go tooling.

title: docs: clarify native enforcement advisory status with examples
body:
## Goal
Make the native integration advisory/enforced boundary easier for users to understand.

## Suggested files
- native-integrations/README.md
- docs/integrations.md

## Acceptance criteria
- Gives one example of advisory behavior.
- Gives one example of what a host API would need for enforcement.
- Keeps the existing honesty boundary: no hard enforcement claim without a blocking host API.

title: cli: add JSON-output smoke checks for scriptable commands
body:
## Goal
Add a small shared pattern or tests that parse JSON output from commands intended for scripts.

## Suggested files
- tests/test_route_command.py
- tests/test_release_automation.py
- tests/test_next_command.py

## Acceptance criteria
- Covers at least two commands that advertise `--json`.
- Uses `json.loads` on command output.
- Keeps assertions focused on stable top-level keys.

title: benchmark: publish the first AgentPack vs no-context E2E report
body:
## Goal
Run and publish the first dated AgentPack vs no-context E2E outcome report.

## Suggested files
- benchmarks/e2e/README.md
- benchmarks/results/YYYY-MM-DD-e2e-ab.md
- docs/benchmarking.md if status changes

## Acceptance criteria
- Uses the documented `benchmark e2e` and `benchmark e2e-report` workflow.
- Reports task success, validation result, tool calls, token/time proxy if available, and time-to-first-correct-file where measurable.
- Keeps file-selection benchmark claims separate from task-success claims.

title: python: add the next language symbol extraction slice
body:
## Goal
Add one focused symbol extraction slice for Rust, Java, or Kotlin.

## Suggested files
- src/agentpack/analysis/symbols.py
- src/agentpack/summaries/offline.py if summaries should use it
- tests/test_symbols.py

## Acceptance criteria
- Extracts at least functions and one type/class-like construct for one language.
- Adds tests with realistic source snippets.
- Updates docs/limitations.md to describe the supported slice honestly.

title: python: broaden scoped mypy coverage beyond the initial gate
body:
## Goal
Expand mypy coverage from the initial scoped gate to a meaningful additional package area.

## Suggested files
- pyproject.toml
- src/agentpack/core/ or src/agentpack/analysis/

## Acceptance criteria
- Adds a coherent package area or small set of modules to `[tool.mypy].files`.
- `python -m mypy` passes.
- Type fixes stay behavior-preserving unless separately tested.

title: benchmark: expand the public corpus with one new repository slice
body:
## Goal
Add one new public repository slice to the benchmark corpus without overfitting rankings to that repository.

## Suggested files
- benchmarks/public-repos.toml
- benchmarks/results/YYYY-MM-DD-public.md after running the suite
- docs/benchmark-learnings.md if the slice changes tuning decisions

## Acceptance criteria
- Adds real historical tasks with expected files.
- Runs the public benchmark command and records the dated result.
- Documents any ranking changes as generalizable rules, not repo-specific hacks.
